questions.prompt: Who is the primary prophet through whom the revelations in the Doctrine and Covenants were given?
Which section of the Doctrine and Covenants is known as the 'Preface' to the book?
Where was the Church of Jesus Christ organized on April 6, 1830?
Which section outlines many of the basic beliefs and organization of the Church and is sometimes called the 'Articles and Covenants'?
Which priesthood was restored by John the Baptist according to the Doctrine and Covenants?
Which section describes the three degrees of glory in the afterlife?
Which revelation is known as the 'Word of Wisdom'?
Which city is identified in the Doctrine and Covenants as the location of Zion or the New Jerusalem?
Which section contains the vision of the celestial kingdom and the degrees of glory received by Joseph Smith and Sidney Rigdon?
Which building was dedicated in Kirtland, Ohio and associated with several revelations in the Doctrine and Covenants?
Which section includes the phrase 'The glory of God is intelligence'?
Which section contains counsel about priesthood power and the principles of righteousness beginning with 'No power or influence can or ought to be maintained'?
Which section teaches about eternal marriage and the new and everlasting covenant?
Who appeared in the Kirtland Temple to restore the keys of the gathering of Israel according to Doctrine and Covenants 110?
Which heavenly messenger restored the sealing power according to Doctrine and Covenants 110?
Which section explains that all things are governed by law and describes the 'law irrevocably decreed in heaven'?
Which section instructs the Saints to establish a house of learning, a house of order, and a house of God?
Which early leader served as one of the Three Witnesses to the Book of Mormon and received revelations in the Doctrine and Covenants?
Which section describes the organization of priesthood quorums including the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles?
Which location in Illinois became the gathering place of the Saints and the setting for several later revelations in the Doctrine and Covenants?
